name: Basic Curry Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 inch piece root ginger peeled and sliced
2 Fleshy green chiles of your choice, seeded and chopped
4 tbsp Plain passata (smooth, thick, sieved tomatoes)
recipeInstructions:
This is the basis for many of the restaurant-style curries you'll find. The curry recipes use half or two-thirds of the amount this recipe gives. If you have leftovers, you could use them in a vegetable curry or freeze them. It will keep in good condition in the freezer but only for a few weeks. Remember to wrap it well so your ice-cream doesn't take on a strange taste!
A. Heat the oil in a heavy-bottomed pan over medium-high heat, then add the chopped onion and stir until translucent.
B. Add the ginger, garlic, green chile, turmeric, cumin, and coriander. Stir for 30 seconds, then reduce the heat to low.
C. Cook for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, ensuring nothing browns or burns.
D. Turn off the heat and let cool slightly. Transfer the contents of the pan to a blender with 4 fl oz cold water and blend until very smooth.
E. Return the puréed mixture to the pan, add salt to taste, and cook for 30 minutes over low heat, stirring occasionally to prevent it from catching on the bottom (add a little hot water if needed).
